methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Bedrock Ocean is a Series A-stage company in the Ocean & Maritime sector founded by Anthony DiMare and Charles Chiau. The company has raised $63M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of directly comparable public or private peers in this specialized sector, limiting the applicability of traditional multiples-based approaches.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology provides the primary analytical anchor. Using total capital raised of $63M as a baseline proxy for current enterprise value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-Series B step-ups yields an implied valuation of $126M. This methodology reflects market convention for risk-adjusted capital efficiency at this stage but lacks corroborating comparable-company evidence.

The triangulation analysis, constrained by the single applicable method, supports a range of $107M (low) to $145M (high), with a median of $126M. The $38M spread reflects inherent model uncertainty rather than methodological diversity. Key valuation drivers remain under-specified: revenue generation, unit economics, addressable market sizing in Ocean & Maritime, and technology maturity. The absence of sector comparables materially limits confidence in this range. Future refinement requires either identified private-market transactions in adjacent maritime-technology investments or clarification of Bedrock Ocean's revenue profile and growth trajectory relative to downstream financing requirements.
